headline: FLOOR-LIMITED CONFIRMATION + METHODOLOGY-NOTE CANDIDATE. Composite 8.8 (near-floor) holds. Continuing in-window evidence: 37 international humanitarian org licenses not renewed (Jan 2026 non-renewal, March 2026 wind-down deadline); aid down 37% Jan-March vs same ceasefire period; one in three health facilities threatened with closure; all five severe-acute-malnutrition treatment centers at risk; UN Working Group cited Israel for denying women/girls protection under international law; OHCHR April 2026 confirmed famine in Gaza governorate. April 23 OCHA: aid entry surged April 14-20 with Zikim Crossing reopening (partial mitigation, already absorbed). April 28 UNSC Gaza ceasefire briefing held. Amnesty 2026 quasi-judicial finding remains institutional anchor. No 5pt-threshold-crossing acute new event in window — sustained negative evidence at near-floor.

evidence.finding: 37 international humanitarian organizations had operating licenses non-renewed; wind-down deadline March 2026.
Aid down 37% Jan-March 2026 vs same ceasefire period; health facility closure threat; SAM treatment center risk.

evidence.source: UN Security Council Monthly Forecast April 2026
evidence.finding: April 28 UNSC Gaza ceasefire briefing scheduled; sustained institutional attention.
Older people in Gaza overlooked health crisis amid blockade of aid and medicines.

methodologyNote: Israel is in floor-limitation cluster (composite 8.8 near-floor). Each dimension at 1.1-1.8. Cumulative evidence weight (Amnesty 2026 quasi-judicial finding, UN famine confirmation, 37 NGO suspension, aid-collapse data) cannot register further downward movement. Recommended inclusion in consolidated floor-limitation methodology-note cycle.
negativeSignals: Sustained near-floor evidence: famine confirmation, aid collapse, NGO suspension, health-facility threat. Mitigation: April 14-20 aid surge with Zikim Crossing reopening (partial).
positiveSignals: Partial: April 14-20 Zikim Crossing aid surge.
